author: @jasonwitt8619
text: The Air hockey table turned upside down, Awesome mate,

author: @marieclaudebedard6728
text: My parents used too have an actual vaccum cleaner that moved around on this principle instead of using wheels. It also had the coolest look! It was a Hoover Constallation and I always thought that using the outgoing air of the machine to help move it was rather brilliant! Nice project anyway, thank you!

replies.author: @will_doherty
replies.text: We had one of those too, as I'm sure did Robert's parents. The thing about the Constellation that I always thought, was that the power used to make the machine effectively levitate would have been better used increasing the vacuum. Not that there was a lack of noticeable suction, it the levitation just seemed a little pointless...

author: @Gwydion67
text:
"One is a Hoover, and there's another one." 😉
I didn't know the brand Hoover much before, but the "other one", a Dyson. 😄👍
Served me well for the last 20 years, now falling apart more and more. And (beware of pan-alert) it still sucks! 😜

Anyway, lovely idea for a second life... 😇

author: @michaelsams6873
text: How do you come up with these fabulous ideas?  "Oh, I see (this) on the side of the road, let's make something"?...Amazing!

author: @BiosWars
text:
Creating a hover-board is not a problem, keeping it powered is, even batteries would not suffice. This is the same issue that every unique futuristic invention faces. How do you create a power supply that can harness and output the desired energy density for extremely long duration's?
And don't forget, it needs to be lightweight and really powerful.. Just something to think about.

author: @craigmccarthy9484
text: Air hockey

author: @NorthernKitty
text:
LOL!!  Your videos always get me looking at my appliances and such with a hope that maybe they'll break down soon!!   😄
After this project, I've got a hungry eye turned upon my leaf-blower.  If a vacuum cleaner motor can do so well, I wonder what my leaf blower motor can do...  😋

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - your leaf blower motor can do awesome things lol

author: @hempev
text: You don't keep the dead skin? How do you get new DNA to clone?

author: @motormaker
text: I use one with a small plywood box to make a forge blower for some amateur black smithing.  It worked well.  I was not aware that they were universal motors. A dc motor control would have been much easier than the full rpm with dump valve arrangement that I fabbricobbled together.

author: @ShadowGPSDFX
text: I made one a few years back with a leaf blower motor. My kids had a blast riding it.

author: @MrTubeuser12
text: if you make it a hovercraft, you will definitely have to add a skirt, other wise it won't cope too well even with just rough ground, also adding a skirt will increase the surface area a bit so you will be able to lift more weight at any given pressure.  basically 1 psi across 1 square inch will lift about 1 pound.   if you come across any electric leaf-blowers, they should work a treat.

author: @lightcapmath2777
text: Another excellent demonstration on recycling parts to make an interesting project. await to see it final project. Keep up the great videos. DVD:)

author: @BearerOfLightSonOfGod
text: Wonder if I can make a ultra light motor but strong as that motor?  Instead of using copper wire maybe a carbon thread or even a graphene thread. The shell can be made of an aluminum foam. Ultrasonic sonic cleaners can dissolve metal foils turning them into micro particles this then can be made into a foam but how? What would I use to hold it together maybe a cryo Gel.

author: @patrickcorroll1735
text: When I was about 10, I got an old school hover and put it on a ply sheet. I put a skirt on the bottom with some holes, to allow the air out and a chair. I got 4 mates on it and had plenty of fun for weeks. Pat

author: @ArbitraryOnslaught
text: you could run it through a catcher/cushion around the bottom. it might increase efficiency. Clever as always Robert!

author: @DavidKenny64
text:
You said "what weight that will carry is going to depend on how many of these you put in and how much voltage".  
How much area is under the "plenum chamber" is probably the biggest factor.  Make it refrigerator size and you could lift a refrigerator with the one motor.

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text:
I don't think so. If you reverse polarity  you will not reverse the direction of the motor. It'll keep spinning the same direction. Please correct me if I'm wrong. If it does reverse it's not a series motor but it'll have a permanent magnet as stator.

Do make the personal hover craft ! That should work but you'll need a flat surface to ride on. Mayne a gym or something. An indoor socker field. Forget about the parking lot, you'll need so many motors you'll take of until the plug is pulled.
